## Key Metadata: eDNA Metabarcoding

*From field collection to taxonomy — what to capture at each step*

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {style="color:white; padding:12px; border-radius:8px; margin-bottom:8px;"}
⛵ **Field Sampling**

- Date, Coordinates, Environmental measurements
:::

::: {style="color:white; padding:12px; border-radius:8px;"}
🧪 **Biomarker / PCR**

- Primer sequence, primer reference, target gene, PCR conditions, length of product, concentration, 
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {style="color:white; padding:12px; border-radius:8px; margin-bottom:8px;"}
🧬 **DNA Extraction**

- Concentration, Extraction method, Extracted amount
:::

::: {style="color:white; padding:12px; border-radius:8px;"}
💻 **Sequences & Taxonomy**

- Sequencing platform, Library layout, Public repositry ID (NCBI, ENA)
- Taxonomy, annotation confidence and reference, Pipeline, Reference library
:::
:::

::: {style="font-size:0.65em; color:#5A8FA8; text-align:center; margin-top:6px;"}
*Category 1 — DNA-derived occurrences · `basisOfRecord = MaterialSample`*
:::

::::

::: notes
At field sampling: map to eventID, decimalLatitude/Longitude, samplingProtocol, Env_broad_scale. At DNA extraction: Concentration and ratioOfAbsorbance fields. At PCR stage: target_gene, pcr_primer_forward/reverse, pcr_cond. At sequences/taxonomy: seq_meth, DNA_sequence, scientificName, taxonID, otu_db.
:::
